text1:
The Caspian Sea-class frigate has been continually upgraded over it's centuries of service, been one of the few designs in service through the Second Committee to the current day. 
Like all GMS products, the Caspian Sea is reliable and easy to service, a dependable lineship that is  capable in both fleet combat and smaller-scale engagements. It is equipped with an advanced sensor suite that allows it to pick out a target for the rest of the fleet, providing precision locks on key structural strongpoints.

The reactor technology that spawned spinally-mounted long-cycle ship weapons also ushered in an age of short-pulse energy weapons. Short-cycle lance batteries are an archetypal example of these. Though mid-ranged, they are accurate and quick at boring holes through hull and armor plate, and can even put some pressure on energy shielding.
A temporary redirection of computing power and sensor arrays towards a particular enemy vessel allows for the creation and broadcast of optimised firing solutions throughout the fleet.
